title: Enthusiasts build an interactive online Listening Museum of iconic mechanical keyboard audio samples 36 mechanical masterpieces available for you to audibly try out
summary: The Listening Museum features 36 multisampled iconic and modern classic keyboards, allowing users to interactively explore and compare their distinct audio samples.
insight: Preserving keyboard sound heritage through immersive multisampling technology.

title: Apple CEO Tim Cook steps down, company names Ternus as incoming CEO
summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook is stepping down on September 1. He will be replaced by John Ternus, the company's hardware engineering lead. Ternus will take the reins on September 1.
insight: Tech giant Apple's leadership transition impacts global innovation landscape

title: New cost-effective DDR5 memory 'HUDIMMs' show around 50% reduction in throughput with single subchannel Two HUDIMMs are as fast as a single stick of regular DDR5 RAM
summary: HUDIMMs are a proposed cost-effective DDR5 memory spec with reduced performance. By using a single 32-bit subchannel, HUDIMMs decrease bandwidth and performance by half. Two HUDIMMs can match the performance of a single regular DDR5 stick.
insight: Reducing bandwidth halves performance in cost-saving memory technology design

title: New Jersey datacenter expansion got $77m in tax breaks to create exactly one permanent job JPMorgan's site already scored $35m and currently employs just 25 workers
summary: A New Jersey data center expansion received $77m in tax breaks despite creating only one new job, adding to concerns about corporate tax incentives that fail to deliver on promised employment. This came after JPMorgan's site already secured $35m in tax breaks and currently employs just 25 workers.
insight: Tax breaks often don't translate to promised job creation.

title: Testing PC games using FEX on a high-end Android tablet can yield playable results but the early tech is still not ready for prime time
summary: FEX translation layer brings PC games to ARM64 devices like Android tablets, yielding playable results, but early technology is still in development.
insight: Early adoption of FEX faces technical hurdles and performance limitations.

title: Chinese chipmakers made record profit in 2025, despite slipping margins U.S shipments fall 34% as Beijing shores up local chipmaking efforts
summary: Chinese chipmakers saw record profit despite slipping margins, driven by U.S. sales. Applied Materials, Lam Research, and KLA collectively booked $19 billion in China revenue across their fiscal 2025 reporting periods. Local chipmaking efforts are gaining momentum in Beijing.
insight: Beijing's chipmaking efforts gain momentum, impacting U.S. sales significantly

title: AI cloud company Vercel breached after employee grants AI tool unrestricted access to Google Workspace hacker seeking $2 million for stolen data
summary: Vercel, a cloud company, was breached after an employee granted access to a hacker. The breach exposed non-sensitive environment variables. A threat actor known as ShinyHunters has claimed responsibility.
insight: Employee actions exposing company data to unauthorized access is a significant risk.

title: Every SK hynix employee could receive $477,000 bonuses this year, almost $900,000 next year 35,000 workers reportedly set to benefit from share of $169 billion projected operating profit
summary: SK hynix plans to distribute a significant portion of its operating profit to employees through performance-based bonuses. Approximately 35,000 employees are expected to receive substantial payouts. The bonuses could reach $477,000 this year and nearly $900,000 next year.
insight: Employees receive significant bonuses tied to company performance and profits

title: Linux 7.1 update includes new in-kernel NTFS driver delivers storage support upgrade for Linux users
summary: Linux 7.1 introduces a new in-kernel NTFS driver for improved storage support.
insight: Native NTFS support enhances compatibility with Windows file systems.

title: Latest Intel Core Ultra 7 270K bundle includes a 2TB SSD and 64GB of RAM for just $1,499 includes Z890 motherboard, case, and AIO cooler, just add a GPU and PSU for a complete PC build
summary: The Newegg Intel combo bundle offers a powerful PC build at $1,499, including a 270K processor, 64GB RAM, 2TB SSD, Z890 motherboard, case, and AIO cooler.
insight: Affordable high-end PC build option for gamers and content creators.

title: US gov't blocks China's largest LED chipmaker's $239 million bid to acquire Dutch lighting firm Lumileds US blocks acquisition attempt of European firm
summary: Sanan Optoelectronics, China's largest LED chipmaker, has abandoned its $239 million bid to acquire Dutch firm Lumileds after the US government blocked the deal through the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States (CFIUS).
insight: US maintains control over foreign acquisitions of strategic US assets

title: Apex Gaming PCs recalls nearly 18,000 power supplies over missing safety labels the fix is a warning sticker sent by mail, units are entirely safe
summary: Apex Gaming PCs recalls 17,730 power supplies due to missing safety labels. The units are entirely safe, but users are advised to apply a warning sticker. The recall was issued by the U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission on April 16.
insight: Safety oversight can lead to costly product recalls and consumer warnings.

title: Relive the PC magazine cover disk era with 758-strong archive.org CD-ROM collection 1.2TB treasure trove also includes Floppy Disks from as early as 1993
summary: The Internet Archive has curated a 1.2TB collection of PC cover disks and floppy disks from 1993, featuring 758 CD-ROMs and offering a nostalgic look at the early days of computing.
insight: Preserving digital history for future generations of tech enthusiasts.

title: Tokyo court rules movie and anime 'spoiler articles' are copyright infringement in landmark criminal case detailed, monetized plot summaries land man in Japanese prison
summary: Tokyo's District Court has ruled 'spoiler articles' as copyright infringement, impacting IP holders.
insight: Detailed plot summaries can be seen as adaptations without permission.

title: UGV Beast An off-road tracked AI robot built for Raspberry Pi 4/5
summary: The Waveshare UGV Beast is an off-road robot designed for Raspberry Pi 4/5 using tracked wheels for improved terrain navigation with AI vision and strategy planning through the ESP32 sub-controller.
insight: Advances autonomous navigation in rugged terrain with AI-driven precision.

title: Ezurio Tungsten 510/700 SMARC SoM features MediaTek Genio 510/700 AIoT SoC, dual GbE, WiFi 6
summary: Ezurio Tungsten 510/700 is a SMARC module powered by MediaTek Genio 510/700 AIoT SoCs, offering high-performance computing and AI capabilities.
insight: MediaTek Genio 510/700 AIoT SoC enables efficient edge computing and IoT applications.

title: LILYGO T-Watch Ultra An IP65-rated ESP32-S3 smartwatch with 2.01-inch AMOLED, LoRa, and GNSS
summary: The LILYGO T-Watch Ultra is an ESP32-S3-based smartwatch with a 2.01-inch AMOLED display, IP65 rating, and features like LoRa, GNSS, and edge AI sensing capabilities. It has a high-capacity 1,100mAh battery and a range of I/O interfaces for development. The watch targets various applications, including GPS tracking and custom smartwatch firmware development.
insight: IoT development becomes more accessible with a powerful, water-resistant smartwatch platform.

title: Edge AI survey finds most pilots fail to reach full rollout
summary: A survey by Fierce Sensors found Edge AI rollout failures due to AI model lifecycle challenges and hardware/connection concerns.
insight: Edge AI adoption hindered by AI model lifecycle complexity issues.

title: TDK Invensense rides again! Plus, Sensors Converge Q&As
summary: TDK InvenSense hosts Sensors Converge, a key industry event. Fierce conducted Q&As with featured speakers, capturing insights from the event. The Q&As highlight the latest trends and innovations in the industry.
insight: Accelerometers, gyroscopes, and microphones converge in future sensing technologies.

title: Q&A: Prasanthi Yerra on CMOS image sensors for surgical views
summary: Prasanthi Yerra discusses CMOS image sensors for improved surgical views, addressing electrical isolation and image performance.
insight: High-fidelity surgical vision enhances precision and reduces medical complications.

title: What s going on with Edge AI? And why a Fierce Sensors survey?
summary: A Fierce survey explores Edge AI pilot success rates, aiming to identify common pitfalls that hinder production. The survey focuses on engineers' experiences to gather insights on what contributes to Edge AI projects' failure to reach production. The results may help organizations improve their AI development processes.
insight: Edge AI pilot success hinges on timely data processing and validation.

title: Tim Cook to step down as Apple CEO, hardware chief John Ternus takes the helm
summary: Tim Cook is stepping down as Apple CEO on September 1, with John Ternus taking over.
insight: Apple's leadership transition may signal a shift in product priorities.

title: Chipmakers on track to meet only 60% of AI memory demand by 2027
summary: Chipmakers face a tight market due to ongoing memory shortages. The shortage is expected to last until they meet demand for AI-focused components from hyperscale customers. This has significant implications for various industries.
insight: Memory shortage limits AI development and innovation potential worldwide

title: If Apple built a $299 "Neo" desktop PC, Windows would have a real problem
summary: Apple's $599 MacBook Neo laptop has disrupted the PC market by entering the sub-$700 segment, surprising industry competitors and potentially threatening Windows PC sales.
insight: Apple's affordability entry shakes up traditional Windows PC market dominance.

title: The Internet Archive makes 758 classic PC Gamer demo discs available to the public
summary: The Internet Archive has made 758 classic PC Gamer demo discs available online, providing a nostalgic trip for gamers. This collection includes snippets of new games that were not available for rent, allowing users to experience them without spending extra money. The archive is a treasure trove of PC gaming history.
insight: Preserving gaming history and nostalgic value for PC gamers worldwide.

title: Asrock's new HUDIMM standard wants to make DDR5 affordable again, by cutting it in half
summary: Asrock's HUDIMM aims to make DDR5 more affordable by reducing its size.
insight: Cost-effective DDR5 solution for system integrators and users.

title: Google Pixel 10 drops to $549, its lowest price yet
summary: The Google Pixel 10 has dropped to its lowest price of $549. This 128GB model features a Tensor chip, 120Hz OLED display, and long-term software support. It's a competitive option in the mainstream segment.
insight: Affordable flagship experience at a lower price point

title: 72 sticks of server RAM were headed for the trash. They're now worth $20,000
summary: 72 sticks of DDR4 RAM were destined for the trash but found new value. An employee saved them, and they eventually sold for $20,000. This highlights the potential for repurposing IT assets.
insight: Proper asset management can yield significant financial returns

title: Researchers are using ultrasound to trigger smell directly in the brain for VR
summary: Researchers are using ultrasound to stimulate the brain's olfactory system directly, enabling the simulation of smells in virtual reality and potentially revolutionizing the field by incorporating a long-missing sensory dimension.
insight: Enhancing emotional realism in VR through multisensory stimulation

title: The first CD recorder was shockingly expensive guess how much
summary: The first CD recorder, made by Denon in 1991, was extremely expensive. Recording a CD cost a small fortune before CDs went mainstream. This made CD technology inaccessible to many consumers.
insight: Early CD technology was unaffordable for the masses initially.

title: Flagship Rematch: Ryzen 7 5800X3D vs. Core i9-12900K
summary: The Ryzen 7 5800X3D and Core i9-12900K remain closely matched in benchmarks, but memory type now significantly impacts performance.
insight: Memory choice greatly affects performance in modern CPU comparisons.
